Fix orchestrator.py equity-aware sizing: current LEVEL/TRENDLINE quality-tier ladder uses fixed qty (22/20/15/10/5/3 contracts) regardless of initial_equity. v42 validator shows 16/20 missed_week trades breached Rule 6 risk cap (worst: 422% of equity on 47 Safe account). Fix: read initial_equity and per_trade_risk_cap_pct from params; compute max_notional = equity * cap_frac; then max_qty = floor(max_notional / (entry_premium * 100)); cap tier qty at min(tier_qty, max_qty). Implement equity-aware qty cap in backtest/lib/orchestrator.py: After simulate_trade_real() returns a fill, if fill.entry_px * 100 * trade_qty > initial_equity * per_trade_risk_cap_pct, scale qty down to floor(initial_equity * per_trade_risk_cap_pct / (fill.entry_px * 100)). Min 3 contracts. Add per_trade_risk_cap_pct as run_backtest() kwarg (default=None = no cap). This fixes Rule 6 breach found by v42_sizing_risk_cap_guard.py: 16/20 missed_week trades used 22 contracts at 100-422% of equity cap. Test: v42 gym validator should pass with cap enabled.

Rolling win-rate sizing analysis for BEARISH_REVERSAL: GOLDILOCKS regime classifier failed (0 IS trades tagged). Test alternative: rolling_wr_sizing. Hypothesis: if the last K=15 IS trades had WR>=55%, scale up 1.5x; if WR<=35%, scale down 0.5x. CRITICAL TENSION: April 2026 catastrophe followed profitable February 2026 (+3123) - rolling WR going into April would be HIGH (scaling up INTO the crash). May 2026 OOS profitable period followed catastrophic March+April - rolling WR going into OOS would be LOW (scaling down DURING recovery). Predict: rolling WR sizing will show IS_delta>0 (reduces catastrophic losses) but OOS_delta<0 (reduces profitable recovery period) = negative WF. If confirmed: (1) write this as a confirmed anti-pattern in docs/LESSONS-LEARNED.md; (2) conclude that performance-based regime classifiers fail for BEARISH_REVERSAL because profitable periods immediately follow catastrophic ones; (3) per-day kill switch is the correct protection. Also test ASYMMETRIC version: only scale DOWN when WR<35%, never scale UP. Does asymmetric version survive OOS? Analyze the April 2026 trade sequence: at what trade # does rolling WR drop below 35%? STRUCTURAL INSIGHT C22 — forward-looking alternatives to backward classifiers for BEARISH_REVERSAL: We just refuted 3 backward-looking classifiers (GOLDILOCKS regime L118, rolling-WR sizing L119, consecutive-stop cooldown L120). The per-day kill switch (dollar-magnitude based) is the correct protection. DESIGN QUESTION: What forward-looking signals could identify high-risk sessions BEFORE they become catastrophic? EXIT-REDESIGN SWEEP (the unlock): The 2026-06-18 watcher validation (analysis/recommendations/level-family-validation.json + breakout-family-validation.json) found that FLOOR_HOLD (SPY-space WR 74.7%), CLOSE_CEILING (54.7%), DOUBLE_BOTTOM_MORNING (73.7%), and NAMED_LEVEL_SECOND_TEST all have positive SPY-price edge but NEGATIVE real-fills expectancy. Root cause = R:R/theta mismatch: chart-stop-only (premium_stop=-0.99) + chart-stop-below-level eats large absolute premium losses the SPY-price proxy never models (lesson C1/C3). TASK: for each of these 4 watchers, sweep the exit knobs to find a config that turns REAL-FILLS expectancy positive: (a) strike offset ATM vs ITM-1 vs ITM-2 (less theta/gamma decay on the loser leg), (b) TP1 premium target +30/+50/+75/+100%, (c) premium-stop -15/-20/-25% INSTEAD of chart-stop-only, (d) trail width. Use validate_level_family.py / validate_breakout_family.py harness with --realfills, OPRA cache valid through 2026-05-29. Gate: real-fills exp>0 AND anchor-no-regression (j_edge_tracker). Write PROMOTE-READY scorecard to analysis/recommendations/ for any config that clears. This is higher-value than building new patterns - it makes the EXISTING validated-edge patterns tradeable.

ANCHOR-ALIGNMENT / REGIME-GATE analysis: The 2026-06-18 validation found NAMED_LEVEL_SECOND_TEST (real-fills +$5.10/trade) and STAIRSTEP_CONTINUATION (real-fills +$4.11) are the ONLY two watchers with positive real-fills, BUT both FAIL OP-16 anchor-no-regression: they LOSE money on J's WIN days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04 - all PUT days, down moves) and MAKE money on his LOSS days. They are structurally ANTI-J-edge: bounce/mean-reversion longs that fight the bearish-continuation trend J actually profits from (C24/C28). TASK: (1) Quantify the regime split - do these patterns only lose on STRONG-TREND-DOWN days (where a bounce-long is doomed)? (2) Test a REGIME GATE: suppress these long/bounce patterns when ribbon=BEAR AND VIX rising AND SPY below VWAP (i.e. don't catch falling knives on J's edge days). (3) Re-run anchor gate with the regime filter - does edge_capture flip positive? (4) Separately: do the BEARISH continuation variants (STAIRSTEP short-side only, close_ceiling short) ALIGN with J's edge - test short-only versions on the anchor WIN days. Hypothesis: J's edge is bearish trend-continuation; the engine should add SHORT-side level patterns, not long bounces. Write findings to strategy/candidates/.

FIX the analyst->chef handoff (doubly broken, drops reflection->R&D routing most days). Two breaks: (1) the EOD analyst runs on the free-tier ladder (Nemotron/DeepSeek/MiniMax) via eod_fallback.py which has NO Write tool, so _chef-inbox routing + _analyst-log.jsonl + mistakes.md appends are silently skipped (eod_fallback.py:314-319 hardcodes them as dropped). (2) Even on the Claude/Write path, the analyst writes strategy/candidates/_chef-inbox/{date}-{slug}.md but kitchen_daemon.py:58 only reads cook-queue.jsonl and NEVER reads _chef-inbox/. So inbox items sit inert. FIX options: (a) give eod_fallback.py a deterministic Python append to cook-queue.jsonl when an inbox item is warranted (no Write tool needed - direct file append), OR (b) add a scheduled _chef-inbox -> cook-queue.jsonl promoter task, OR (c) escalate the analyst to the Write-capable Claude path when routing is needed. Recommend (a) - simplest, no new scheduled task. Verify the EOD digest's recommendations actually reach cook-queue.

Do this BEFORE re-enabling shadow mode.

STAIRSTEP_CONTINUATION eval-first redesign (currently WATCH_ONLY / DO-NOT-PROMOTE). The detector as shipped has two defects: (1) its docstring + the v45 gym fixture use FABRICATED bar values (736.12/735.61/735.41) that do NOT exist in the real 2026-05-07 tape - the real descending highs pressing 735.40 are 735.59/735.55/735.50/735.39; the detector fires 0 times on the REAL anchor. (2) the _collect_descending_retests local-maximum requirement (h>prev AND h>=next) can never be satisfied by a clean consecutive descending staircase, so the actual stairstep highs get filtered out. (3) CRITICAL: 5/07 is a J LOSS day - every tested logic fix (collect-all-near-level, rejection-bar filter) made the detector MORE anti-J-edge (edge_capture -647 vs as-shipped -365, profits on J loss days). TASK: redesign via the eval-first pipeline - first establish whether ANY stairstep-continuation variant has positive real-fills AND positive edge_capture (does not profit on J loss days). If none does, RETIRE the detector honestly rather than ship a fabricated-fixture green test. Fix the docstring + v45 fixture to use real tape regardless.
